data.title: Benitec Biopharma files to sell 900K shares of common stock for holders
data.description:
• Benitec Biopharma (NASDAQ:BNTC) filed to sell 900K shares of common stock for holders
  • Filing

data.title: Triller Group receives delinquency notice from Nasdaq
data.description:
• Triller Group (NASDAQ:ILLR) received a delinquency notice from Nasdaq on May 20, 2025, for failing to file its quarterly report for the period ended March 31, 2025 on time.
  • Triller must submit a compliance plan by June 16, 2025; if accepted, it may get an extension until October 13, 2025 to file.
  • If Nasdaq rejects the plan, Triller can appeal the decision to a Nasdaq Hearings Panel.
  • ILLR shares up 5.3% post-market on Friday.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Tandem to pay Roche $36M in patent dispute settlement
data.description:
Roche (OTCQX:RHHBY) (OTCQX:RHHBF) and Tandem Diabetes Care (NASDAQ:TNDM) have reached a settlement agreement to resolve all ongoing and potential patent disputes concerning Roche's two European patents and Tandem's t:slim X2 insulin pump.

In total, Tandem will pay Roche $36M over five years, beginning with the initial $8M payment, followed by four equal annual installments.

 In exchange, both Tandem and Roche have granted each other 10-year cross-licenses to their respective insulin delivery system patents and patent applications, Tandem said in a filing.

data.title: urban-gro receives Nasdaq non-compliance notification
data.description:
• urban-gro (NASDAQ:UGRO) on Friday said it has received a notice from Nasdaq, stating that because the company had not yet filed its Quarterly Report on Form 10-Q for the fiscal quarter ended March 31, 2025 and its Annual Report on Form 10-K for the fiscal quarter ended December 31, 2024, it continues to be out of compliance with listing rules.
  • This notification has no immediate effect on the listing of the company's common stock on Nasdaq.
  • UGRO +4.87% after hours to $0.3765.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Amazon sued for selling contaminated rice
data.description:
Amazon (NASDAQ:AMZN) has been named the defendant in a class action lawsuit alleging the company sold rice contaminated with arsenic and heavy metals.

A study conducted by Healthy Babies, Bright Futures found toxic chemicals in 18 types of rice sold on the e-commerce giant’s digital marketplace.

The study found that more than one in four rice samples contained arsenic levels above the FDA’s 100 ppb “Action Level” for infant rice cereal. As a result, the U.S. Food and Drug Administration can deem these products as adulterated if they exceed this threshold.

The plaintiff’s lawsuit accuses Amazon (NASDAQ:AMZN) of knowingly selling these brands on its website, including its own Whole Food’s 365 brand, without testing it for heavy metals.

According to Reuters, the lawsuit seeks unspecified damages of at least $5M for violations of Washington state consumer protection laws.

data.title: Midland States Bancorp receives deficiency notification from Nasdaq
data.description:
• Midland States Bancorp (NASDAQ:MSBI) received deficiency notification from Nasdaq that MSBI was not in compliance as a result of its failure to timely file its annual report for 2024 and its quarterly report for the quarter ended March 31, 2025.
  • MSBI has until June 2, 2025, to submit to Nasdaq a plan to regain compliance.
  • Pursuant to the notice, Nasdaq has the discretion to grant MSBI up to 180 September 29, 2025, to regain compliance.
  • MSBI intends to take the necessary steps to regain compliance with Nasdaq’s listing rules as soon as practicable.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Captivision receives Nasdaq notice regarding delayed Form 20-F filing
data.description:
• Captivision (NASDAQ:CAPT) announced on Friday that it received a Nasdaq letter, stating that the company is not in compliance with Nasdaq listing rules because it has not yet filed its annual report on Form 20-F for the period ended December 31, 2024, with the Securities and Exchange Commission.
  • The Letter is only a notification of deficiency, not of imminent delisting, and has no current effect on the listing or trading of the company’s securities on Nasdaq.
  • In accordance with Nasdaq listing rules, the company has 60 calendar days (until July 21, 2025) to submit a plan to regain compliance. If Nasdaq accepts the Company’s plan, Nasdaq can grant an exception to up to 180 calendar days from the due date of the Form 20-F, or until November 11, 2025, to regain compliance.
  • CAPT -0.66% after hours to $0.45.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Polyrizon recives Nasdaq delisting notice
data.description:
• Polyrizon (NASDAQ:PLRZ) received a notification letter from Nasdaq to delist PLRZ’s securities pursuant to its discretionary authority.
  • PLRZ’s issuance of securities pursuant to the securities purchase agreement dated March 31, 2025, particularly the Series A warrants exercisable on an alternate cashless basis as described in PLRZ’s prior SEC filings, raises public interest concerns because the issuance resulted in substantial dilution for its shareholders.
  • PLRZ shares up over 2% post-market on Friday.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Viomi Technology receives Nasdaq non-compliance notice
data.description:
• Viomi Technology (NASDAQ:VIOT) received a letter from the Nasdaq that VIOT is not in compliance with the requirements for continued listing because it did not timely file its annual report for the fiscal year ended 2024..
  • If Nasdaq accepts the VIOT's compliance plan, it may grant VIOT an extension until November 11, 2025 to regain compliance.
  • VIOT intends to submit the Compliance Plan within the prescribed 60-day period.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Jayud Global Logistics receives non-compliance notice from Nasdaq
data.description:
• Jayud Global Logistics (NASDAQ:JYD) received a notification letter from Nasdaq notifying that the company is not in compliance with the minimum bid price requirement.
  • The closing bid price for the company's ordinary shares listed on the Nasdaq was below $1.00 per share for 30 consecutive business days.
  • The notice provides that the Company has a period of 180 calendar days or until November 11, 2025, to regain compliance.
  • Source: Press Release
